checks.name: doctrine_coherence
checks.description: Every clause in VISUAL_DIRECTION_DOCTRINE traces to at least one support point in EXECUTIVE_COGNITION_MODEL, ANTI_DASHBOARD_ENFORCEMENT, or VISUAL_EVALUATION_RUBRIC.
checks.notes: Cross-references explicitly named in each document; no orphan clauses.

checks.name: non_conflict_with_governance
checks.description: No doctrine clause contradicts git_structure_contract.md or reference_boundary_contract.md.
checks.notes: Doctrine scope is explicitly L7 (51.x); no claim of upstream authority. Doctrine §14 names governance precedence.

checks.name: compatibility_with_lens_v2_flagship_architecture
checks.description: Doctrine does not require structural changes incompatible with current PI.LENS.V2.FLAGSHIP-EXPERIENCE-ELEVATION.01 / PI.LENS.V2.EXPERIENCE-REFINEMENT.01 outcomes.
checks.notes: Doctrine is direction, not implementation override. Existing flagship route remains valid; future contracts evaluate against the rubric.

checks.name: anti_dashboard_consistency
checks.description: Five-second confusion, grid-gravity, metric-first, and component-repetition rules are stated identically in ANTI_DASHBOARD_ENFORCEMENT and referenced in VISUAL_EVALUATION_RUBRIC.
checks.notes: Tests defined in ANTI_DASHBOARD_ENFORCEMENT §6-§9; rubric axis E references the same.

checks.name: typography_first_alignment
checks.description: Typography-first hierarchy is declared in doctrine §5.3, supported in cognition model §4, and tested in rubric axis B.
checks.notes: All three documents agree typography is primary navigation.

checks.name: executive_cognition_consistency
checks.description: The reader described in EXECUTIVE_COGNITION_MODEL is the reader the rubric calibrates against and the doctrine designs for.
checks.notes: Reader profile (senior operator, 5s/30s/90s/5m/10m budget) consistent across all documents.
